reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.308(a)(5)(ii)(B) focuses on protection from malicious software, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security practices. Ensuring that images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment (k8s_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled) helps prevent malicious software from being introduced. Admission controllers can enforce security policies to prevent the deployment of potentially harmful pods (k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled). Applying restrictive pod security standards (k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted) can limit the capabilities of pods, reducing the risk of malicious activity. Network policies that deny ingress by default (k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_ingress) can prevent unauthorized access, further protecting against malicious software.
title: 164_308_a_5_ii_b: 164.308(a)(5)(ii)(B) Protection from malicious software
